epistemic_status: MEASURED — live WDQS snapshot 2026-07-13. Each number is reproducible by re-running the query string, but the LIVE endpoint drifts upward as Wikidata grows; exact pinning requires the dated dump (heavy-ingest step, scoped not run). No feasibility conclusion.
purpose: Replace the increment-2 ~50,000-100,000 non-biological-concept ESTIMATE (ASM-2059) and the decontamination-eligibility estimate with MEASURED counts, CPU-light (HTTP, seconds).
method: curl -G with User-Agent, Accept application/sparql-results+json, 60s timeout; COUNT(DISTINCT ?x) aggregates; bounded property-existence and subtree queries only (unbounded whole-class-layer count TIMES OUT at the 60s WDQS limit — reported as such, not fabricated).

external_id_existence.note: Distinct Wikidata items carrying each external ID = the exact-decontamination FUEL against the existing 207,733-cluster union (each ID resolves an item to a specific local shard record).

P686_chebi_id.resolves_against: data/onto-obo CHEBI (only 41 local → most are NEW chemistry, not collisions)

P685_ncbi_taxon_id.resolves_against: data/onto-obo NCBITaxon (only 402 local → the vast majority are named-individual taxa, excluded from concept count)

external_id_intersect_class_layer.note: External-ID items that ALSO have a P279 (subclass-of) backbone = decontamination-eligible CLASSES (not individuals).

P8814_wordnet_AND_P279.meaning: Wikidata classes exactly joinable to our WordNet shard by synset ID — the cross-domain (mostly non-bio) decontamination set.

P8814_wordnet_AND_P31.meaning: WordNet-linked items with an instance-of edge (individuals or dual-role); reported for the class/individual split.

P686_chebi_AND_P279.meaning: ChEBI-linked classes (bio decontam; nearly all NEW vs our 41 local ChEBI).

P5270_mondo_AND_P279.meaning: MONDO-linked classes (bio decontam against 32,136 local MONDO).

nonbio_domain_class_subtrees_P279star.note: Bounded P279* subtree class counts for non-biological domains. Subtrees OVERLAP (a class can be under several roots) so these are NOT summable; each shows a single non-bio domain already carries tens of thousands of P279-backbone classes. Two subtrees are too large to count within the 60s WDQS limit — itself evidence of a large non-bio layer.

event_Q1190554.count: TIMEOUT>60s (subtree too large to count live)

unbounded_whole_class_layer.classes_total_distinct_P279_subjects: TIMEOUT (HTTP 502 after 17s) — the whole class layer is too large for a live COUNT(DISTINCT) within the WDQS limit; exact total requires the dump. Published Wikidata class-layer size is ~2-3M items [LIT-BACKED, unpinned].
unbounded_whole_class_layer.classes_bio_exclusion_P279star_of_{organ: TIMEOUT — deep transitive closure over the organism root exceeds the limit.

headline_measured_findings: The ~50-100k non-bio-concept estimate (ASM-2059) is now MEASURED-SUPPORTED: single non-bio domains already carry 6,616 (occupation), 50,430 (organization), 63,292 (vehicle) and 103,707 (position) P279-backbone classes; a benchmark-blind slice across non-bio domains reaches and exceeds 100k with room to spare (position alone exceeds it).

Exact decontamination against the existing union is real and BOUNDED: 17,374 Wikidata classes carry a WordNet-3.1 synset ID (P8814∩P279) → exactly joinable to our WordNet shard; ChEBI (43,559) and MONDO (17,631) linked classes cover bio decontam. So of a ~100k non-bio slice only ~17k would collide with our union via WordNet; the remainder are genuinely NEW non-bio concepts — the domain-balance headroom we want.

The bio external-ID counts confirm the local shards are small subsets (627,783 taxon-linked, 43,560 ChEBI-linked items exist in Wikidata vs 402 / 41 local) — but taxa are overwhelmingly named individuals (P31, not P279) and excluded from the concept count.
